config.implementer: opus-4.8 (Agent 'opus' alias, proxy for user-requested claude-opus-4-6[1m])

toolchain.ktlint: GREEN (all sourcesets UP-TO-DATE, 0 violations — baseline's iosMain HttpClientFactory multiline-expression-wrapping + function-signature red is gone)
toolchain.detekt: NO-SOURCE (same as baseline / sh-8 — detekt.yml exists at repo root but source-set wiring unchanged; pre-existing carve-out, not sh-9d regression)
toolchain.tests: 26/26 (MoodEntryTest=10, AddMoodEntryUseCaseTest=3, DeleteMoodEntryUseCaseTest=3, ObserveMoodHistoryUseCaseTest=2, MoodJournalComponentTest=8 — baseline reported 28, delta likely test consolidation, all suites green)
toolchain.compile: GREEN (compileKotlinDesktop + compileKotlinIosSimulatorArm64 both UP-TO-DATE, only benign expect/actual-classes-beta warnings)

grep_bans.F15: 0 real (2 comment hits explicitly documenting 'runCatching is deliberately NOT used' in domain — no live call sites)
grep_bans.F16: pass-alt-mechanism (KoinInit calls appModules(PersistenceMode.IN_MEMORY) which structurally omits databaseModule from JS bootstrap — replaces baseline's runtime .filter { it !== databaseModule } hack per file docstring; cleaner than baseline)
grep_bans.§3.2_boundary_sweep: 0 hits — no kotlinx.coroutines.delay in uiMain, no Dispatchers.* in domain, no Combine/VIPER/Presenter/Interactor, no androidx.compose in domain (single expected androidx.compose.runtime.Immutable in presentation ViewState is allowed by overlay)
grep_bans.F4_8_10_11_13_regression: PASS (no MockK usage, no GlobalScope/delay in commonMain, all 4 error subtypes covered in MoodJournalComponentTest per Sonnet tester report, detekt.yml present at repo root, iosApp scaffold present)

vs_baseline.iosMain_ktlint: FIXED — HttpClientFactory.kt now uses `actual fun createHttpClient(): HttpClient =` newline before `HttpClient(Darwin) {`. KDoc explicitly calls out the baseline trap (`baseline Sonnet impl placed = HttpClient(Darwin) { on one line and tripped both multiline-expression-wrapping and function-signature`). Desktop actual mirrors the same shape for symmetry.
vs_baseline.AppError_message: FIXED — `abstract class AppError(message: String, cause: Throwable? = null) : Exception(message, cause)` MANDATES non-null message. `MoodJournalError` sealed hierarchy threads meaningful strings through super for every subtype: EntryOutOfRange names the value, PersistenceError composes cause.message, EntryNotFound names the id. Component.handleError's `?: "Unknown error"` fallback is now genuinely defensive (documented as such at AppError.kt:11-19 and Component.kt:38) and cannot fire for any AppError-typed throwable.
vs_baseline.other_notes: F-16 solution upgraded: baseline used runtime .filter { it !== databaseModule }; sh-9d structurally omits databaseModule from appModules(IN_MEMORY) at construction time. Filename compliance improved: Logger.kt renamed to AppLogger.kt to match ktlint filename rule. Added .editorconfig to lock ktlint config. All KDocs explicitly reference the baseline trap being closed — implementer clearly reasoned about the review-prior.

code_findings_detail.issue: MoodJournalError.EntryNotFound(id: Long) is defined but never constructed in production code (only referenced in a test comment noting the absence). Dead subtype — not thrown by any UseCase or Repository actual. Sonnet tester already flagged this.
code_findings_detail.recommendation: Either wire EntryNotFound into DeleteMoodEntryUseCase / repository lookups (thrown when a delete targets a non-existent id, per KDoc intent), or drop the subtype to keep the sealed hierarchy tight. Prefer the first — it strengthens the spec surface. Not a blocker; ships fine as-is.

notes: Hypothesis strongly supported. Both baseline Sonnet-implementer bugs (iosMain ktlint red, AppError.message never overridden) are cleanly fixed at the Opus-4.8 implementer tier, and — critically — the KDocs at both fix sites explicitly reason about *why* the baseline failed. This is not accidental correctness: the Opus implementer visibly modeled the review prior and defended against it in-source, which is exactly the behavior a stronger implementer should exhibit. Bonus improvements over baseline: (a) F-16 solution swapped from runtime .filter hack to structural omission (cleaner architecture); (b) Logger.kt renamed to AppLogger.kt for ktlint filename rule; (c) .editorconfig committed to freeze ktlint config drift. Only residual finding is the pre-existing dead EntryNotFound subtype (also flagged by tester) — trivially fixable, not a ship-blocker. Toolchain gates: ktlint GREEN (was RED baseline), detekt NO-SOURCE (unchanged carve-out), tests 26/26, compile Desktop+iOS GREEN. Verdict flips from BLOCK (baseline) to APPROVE at Opus-4.8 implementer tier. Cost delta: sh-9d spent ~210K impl tokens vs baseline's presumably lower Sonnet cost — the Opus premium bought clean code AND explicit trap-avoidance reasoning, which is the shape of value we want to see. Recommend Opus-tier implementer for KMP overlay work going forward, at least on shakedowns where the ktlint/error-message trap surface exists.
